title: Observable — Why Salt Is a Crystal

warnings: [SCIENCE] illustrative-not-simulation disclosure required — state visuals are educational illustration, not simulation output
notes: Row 6: AI-disclosure obligation planned — verify presence at Pre-Publish.
Social = mass dissemination. Platform AI-label + community guidelines apply.
Revise and re-submit to Legal Gate (Gate 0).

science_subject.key_measurement: Lattice parameter a ≈ 5.64 Å (NaCl, COD 1000040); 6:6 coordination
science_subject.visualization_prompt: Scientific crystal lattice visualization, rock-salt NaCl structure, alternating Na+ and Cl- ions on face-centered cubic grid, 6:6 coordination, ball-and-stick with unit cell outline, neutral dark background, accurate relative ion positions, NOT TO SCALE if unit cell repeated, 16:9 documentary fidelity

config.voice_suffix: clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Julian-001: identical presenter face, smart-casual wardrobe, seamless neutral set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter addresses camera on seamless neutral, crisp science hook. Camera: locked medium, direct address. Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. COLOR LOCK Seamless-Neutral: backdrop #9A9A98±2%, skin true-to-life, wardrobe colors accurate, no gel spill — no magenta edge gradient, no orange spotlight cone. GRADE LOCK Cool-Clinical: grey axis neutral, skin neither pink nor yellow pushed, saturation -15% global — forbid magenta tint, forbid orange warmth, forbid teal skin on shadows, forbid vintage fade. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "Table salt looks like a simple white grain — but inside, every speck is a perfectly repeating grid of charged atoms locked by electrostatic glue." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: Table salt looks like a simple white grain — but inside, every speck is a perfectly repeating grid of charged atoms locked by electrostatic glue.
shots.on_screen: Observable · Why Salt Is a Crystal

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Julian-001: identical presenter face, smart-casual wardrobe, seamless neutral set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter explains the phenomenon plainly, motivated gesture, clinical lighting. Camera: slow push-in medium. Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. COLOR LOCK Seamless-Neutral: backdrop #9A9A98±2%, skin true-to-life, wardrobe colors accurate, no gel spill — no magenta edge gradient, no orange spotlight cone. GRADE LOCK Cool-Clinical: grey axis neutral, skin neither pink nor yellow pushed, saturation -15% global — forbid magenta tint, forbid orange warmth, forbid teal skin on shadows, forbid vintage fade. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "Sodium has one electron to lose; chlorine wants one to gain. Transfer makes Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions. Opposites attract — but in a solid, each ion is tugged by neighbors in all directions, settling into a rock-salt lattice: alternating sodium and chlorine on a cubic grid." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: Sodium has one electron to lose; chlorine wants one to gain. Transfer makes Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions. Opposites attract — but in a solid, each ion is tugged by neighbors in all directions, settling into a rock-salt lattice: alternating sodium and chlorine on a cubic grid.

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Julian-001: identical presenter face, smart-casual wardrobe, seamless neutral set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter cites measurement, experiment, or observation chain — evidence-forward tone. Camera: medium — gesture to evidence. Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. COLOR LOCK Seamless-Neutral: backdrop #9A9A98±2%, skin true-to-life, wardrobe colors accurate, no gel spill — no magenta edge gradient, no orange spotlight cone. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "X-ray diffraction maps where atoms sit in a crystal. For sodium chloride, the spacing and the six-neighbor geometry are catalogued in open crystallography databases — COD entry one-zero-zero-zero-zero-four-zero — not inferred from a textbook sketch." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: X-ray diffraction maps where atoms sit in a crystal. For sodium chloride, the spacing and the six-neighbor geometry are catalogued in open crystallography databases — COD entry one-zero-zero-zero-zero-four-zero — not inferred from a textbook sketch.

shots.speech_text: This model shows the repeating unit: purple chloride, gold sodium, each ion touched by six partners. We repeat the cell for clarity — a teaching model, not a microscope photograph of one grain.

shots.speech_text: Ionic crystals are everywhere — table salt, ceramics, battery electrolytes. The same electrostatic accounting that builds salt also sets melting points, hardness, and why shattering a crystal cleaves along flat planes.
